content:
Context: 
Content: Overseas Business Travel Policy
Version: 2.0
28th April 2025
1. General

Context: 1.1 Purpose
Content: [1.1 Purpose]
This policy is to provide administrative guidelines for JRG employees regarding overseas travel
for business purposes.
The principle for the policy is to ensure that employees should neither suffer a financial loss nor
make a gain as a result of overseas business trips. All employees are expected to act
responsibly to ensure that the costs incurred are reasonable during overseas business trips.
Banners and Functions are responsible for the proper approval of business travel. Department
Heads and Managers should make sure the proposed business travels are necessary.

metadata.hierarchy: 1.1 Purpose

content:
Context: 1.2 Procedures
Content: [1.2 Procedures]
1.2.1. Travel Authorisation Form (“TAF”) should be completed by the traveler 30 days prior to
the intended departure date and submit for approval. A copy of the travel plan should be
attached.
1.2.2. Travelers are required to indicate whether the trip is a budgeted trip on the TAF.
1.2.3. Flight bookings, accommodation and other travel arrangements will be made by
Executive Secretaries / Designated personnel once the TAF is approved.
1.2.4. For expense claiming purposes, travelers should provide original receipts. In locations
where receipts are not normally issued, travelers should itemise and provide details to
support the claim.
1.2.5. Expense claims should be submitted within 2 months upon returning from business trip.

2. Air Travel

metadata.hierarchy: 1.3.5 Please refer to the detailed approval requirements and approvers in Appendix 2. > 1.4 Special Circumstances not stipulated in the Policy/ Exceptions > 1.4.3 In case of any doubts, travelers should discuss with their manager or Finance Director.

content:
Context: 1.3.5 Please refer to the detailed approval requirements and approvers in Appendix 2. > 2.1 Entitlement
Content: [2.1 Entitlement]
2.1.1. Frequency of overseas travel should be justified by business needs and add value for
the company.
2.1.2. Air travel should be booked through approved agents by Executive Secretaries /
Designated personnel.
2.1.3. Travel class should follow the table below:
Destination Travel Class
CEO, Brand MDs, Group Function Directors
International > 6 hours Business
International ≤ 6 hours Economy
Domestic Economy
All Other Staff
International > 6 hours Premium Economy
International ≤ 6 hours Economy
Domestic Economy
2.1.4. For overseas travel for training purposes, Economy class should be taken
2.1.5. Team members traveling with suppliers/ business associates may travel in the same class,
subject to prior approval and justified reasons.
2.1.6. Any exceptions to the above should be justified with reasons and obtain prior approval by
TAF the approver(s).
2.2. Fare Class
2.2.1. For short haul flights ≤ 6 hours, air travel should be booked based on the most direct route
at the most economical rate.
2.2.2. The most economical carrier shall be used whenever possible.
2.2.3. When traveling as a group, tickets on group fares should be purchased whenever
available.
2.2.4. For long haul flights, indirect flights with reasonable departure, arrival and transit time
may be booked if substantial savings would be achieved.
2.3. Advanced Booking
2.3.1. Flights should be booked at least 1 month in advance.
2.3.2. For unavoidable emergency trips, at least 3 working days prior to travel, with justified
reasons and approval from TAF approver.
2.4. Traveling with family members/ friends
2.4.1. Traveling expenses arising from family members / friends should be bourne by the
individual.
2.5. Restrictions
2.5.1. Travelers should plan to reach the destination on time for business meetings. For flights
> 6 hours, or where necessary due to flight schedules or travel arrangements, reaching
the destination 1 day ahead is permitted.
2.5.2. After completion of business meetings/ planned agendas, travelers should return to their
base locations on the same day, or the following day if limited by flight schedules or
availability of local transportation.
2.5.3. Travelers should bear the incremental cost for arriving earlier/ staying behind for personal
reasons. Additional time spent should be deducted from the annual leave balance.
2.5.4. Cost of cancellation/ rebooking/ amendment arisen out of the traveler’s personal reasons
shall be bourne by the individual.

[Table Data]:
Destination | Travel Class
CEO, Brand MDs, Group Function Directors International > 6 hours International ≤ 6 hours Domestic | Business Economy Economy
All Other Staff International > 6 hours International ≤ 6 hours Domestic | Premium Economy Economy Economy

2.5.5. It is not permitted to voluntarily downgrade the class of travel to cover incremental costs
of private travel, or expenses of accompanying family/ friends.
2.5.6. No more than 3 members of the same team or 3 executives should travel on the same
flight, whenever possible.
2.5.7. Cost of excess baggage due to personal reasons will be bourne by the traveler.
3. Transportation Arrangements
3.1. Airport Transfer
3.1.1. Local public transport and licenced taxis should be used whenever possible.
3.1.2. Hotel limousines between airport and hotel should only be used if local taxis are
considered unsafe. In such case, the most economical option should be used.
3.1.3. Employees traveling together on the same business trip should share the transport
arrangements.
3.2. Transportation at destination
3.2.1. All transportation expenses directly related to business purposes are reimbursable.
3.2.2. Travelers are normally expected to take standard class on local public transportation
unless with prior approval.
3.2.3. Personal transportation expenses should be borne by the individual.
3.3. Car Rental
3.3.1. Generally, JRG discourages employees from car rentals, unless all the following
conditions are met:
a) The traveler has a valid international driving license and has adequate driving skills to
drive on local roads;
b) It is a more economical option as compared to other forms of local transportation and
taxis;
c) Prior approval was sought from the TAF approvers.
3.3.2. If all conditions are met, the traveler may reimburse the relevant car rental costs,
insurance, fuel and parking fees as incurred.
4. Accommodation Arrangements
4.1. Entitlement
4.1.1. The default room type is Standard Room (single)
4.1.2. Under certain special circumstances, such as large group travel to events and
conferences, shared room may be applied to optimise budget.
4.1.3. In case of room sharing, coworkers of the same gender shall share a room.
4.1.4. Cost of any upgrade will be bourne by the traveler.
4.1.5. Travelers are encouraged to stay at hotels with corporate rates or recommended by the
company.
4.1.6. Staying in private apartments, or other unofficial accommodation is generally not
permitted.
4.1.7. Hotel bills should be first settled by the traveler and then reimbursed through travel
expenses claims, except pre-arranged large group functions sponsored by the
company.
4.1.8. Hotel services for personal entertainment such as mini bar, spa, paid movies will not be
reimbursable.
Meals
5.1. Entitlement
5.1.1. Reasonable meal consumption is reimbursable, the maximum daily meal consumption
amount per person should be referred to Appendix 1.
5.1.2. Overall average expense incurred during the trip should not exceed the daily amount.
5.1.3. Reimbursement begins from date of departure until date of return from the business trip
5.1.4. Alcohol is excluded.
5.1.5. Travelers should claim for their meals based on actual expenses incurred, supported by
original receipts. Should it not been issued, travelers are required to provide details to
justify the claim.
5. Gift and Entertainment during Overseas Travel

Notes:
1) All T&E spend should be made according to the latest T&E policies and travel budget.
2) Budget and Latest Forecast Validation is based on the budgeted trips approved at the
latest Budget/ LF cycles.
3) Unbudgeted/ Overbudget spend, these will require approval from both the relevant
approvers, and Market FD (for GMs & Local Functions)/ FD of the affected region (for
Brand MD & Regional Functions)/ GFD (for Group functions) before spend.
4) Market to process payment based on the approval requirements above.
